diagrams.description: Commercial Law governs business and commercial transactions. This flowchart shows its systematic process: the ultimate goal is to facilitate commerce, ensure fairness, and provide legal certainty. It integrates core areas—contracts, sale of goods, secured transactions, negotiable instruments, business organizations, competition, consumer protection, and international trade. These are underpinned by fundamental principles (freedom of contract, good faith, party autonomy) and derived from multiple sources (statutes such as the UCC, case law, international conventions). The resulting framework provides predictable rules that enable efficient business operations, resolve disputes, and promote economic growth.
